inlineContent.text: Callouts are an important part of the typing experience, where input callouts can show the currently pressed key and action callouts can show secondary actions when certain keys are long pressed.
KeyboardKit has ways to automatically show an
when the user types, and has a
protocol that can provide actions to present in an
when long pressing certain keys.
unlocks localized services for all locales. Information about Pro features can be found at the end of this article.
KeyboardKit has a
namespace that contains callout-related types and views. For instance, an
can present the currently pressed character while an
can present secondary actions when long pressing a key.
KeyboardKit has an observable
class that provides observable input and action callout state, such as the currently pressed key or the callout actions to present.
KeyboardKit automatically creates an instance of this class and injects it into
, then updates this context when keys are pressed.
In KeyboardKit, a
can be used to provide secondary callout actions for any key, which are then presented when a key with actions is long pressed.
KeyboardKit automatically creates an instance of
and injects it into
. You can replace it at any time, as described further down, or inject localized services into it.
You can easily resolve various
implementations with these shorthands, sorted by relevance:

The
namespace has callout-specific views, that can be used to mimic the native iOS & iPadOS input and action callouts.
The
